root_text.an10.23:1.1: “Atthi, bhikkhave, dhammā kāyena pahātabbā, no vācāya.
root_text.an10.23:1.2: Atthi, bhikkhave, dhammā vācāya pahātabbā, no kāyena.
root_text.an10.23:1.3: Atthi, bhikkhave, dhammā neva kāyena pahātabbā no vācāya, paññāya disvā pahātabbā.
root_text.an10.23:2.1: Katame ca, bhikkhave, dhammā kāyena pahātabbā, no vācāya?
root_text.an10.23:2.2: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu akusalaṁ āpanno hoti kiñci desaṁ kāyena.
root_text.an10.23:2.4: ‘āyasmā kho akusalaṁ āpanno kiñci desaṁ kāyena.
root_text.an10.23:2.5: Sādhu vatāyasmā kāyaduccaritaṁ pahāya kāyasucaritaṁ bhāvetū’ti.
root_text.an10.23:2.6: So anuvicca viññūhi sabrahmacārīhi vuccamāno kāyaduccaritaṁ pahāya kāyasucaritaṁ bhāveti.
root_text.an10.23:2.7: Ime vuccanti, bhikkhave, dhammā kāyena pahātabbā, no vācāya.
root_text.an10.23:3.1: Katame ca, bhikkhave, dhammā vācāya pahātabbā, no kāyena?
root_text.an10.23:3.2: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu akusalaṁ āpanno hoti kiñci desaṁ vācāya.
root_text.an10.23:3.4: ‘āyasmā kho akusalaṁ āpanno kiñci desaṁ vācāya.
root_text.an10.23:3.5: Sādhu vatāyasmā vacīduccaritaṁ pahāya vacīsucaritaṁ bhāvetū’ti.
root_text.an10.23:3.6: So anuvicca viññūhi sabrahmacārīhi vuccamāno vacīduccaritaṁ pahāya vacīsucaritaṁ bhāveti.
root_text.an10.23:3.7: Ime vuccanti, bhikkhave, dhammā vācāya pahātabbā, no kāyena.
root_text.an10.23:4.1: Katame ca, bhikkhave, dhammā neva kāyena pahātabbā no vācāya, paññāya disvā pahātabbā?
root_text.an10.23:4.2: Lobho, bhikkhave, neva kāyena pahātabbo no vācāya, paññāya disvā pahātabbo.
root_text.an10.23:4.9: macchariyaṁ, bhikkhave, neva kāyena pahātabbaṁ no vācāya, paññāya disvā pahātabbaṁ.
root_text.an10.23:5.1: Pāpikā, bhikkhave, issā neva kāyena pahātabbā no vācāya, paññāya disvā pahātabbā.
root_text.an10.23:5.3: Idha, bhikkhave, ijjhati gahapatissa vā gahapatiputtassa vā dhanena vā dhaññena vā rajatena vā jātarūpena vā.
root_text.an10.23:5.4: Tatrāññatarassa dāsassa vā upavāsassa vā evaṁ hoti:
root_text.an10.23:5.5: ‘aho vatimassa gahapatissa vā gahapatiputtassa vā na ijjheyya dhanena vā dhaññena vā rajatena vā jātarūpena vā’ti.
root_text.an10.23:5.6: Samaṇo vā pana brāhmaṇo vā lābhī hoti cīvarapiṇḍapātasenāsanagilānapaccayabhesajjaparikkhārānaṁ.
root_text.an10.23:5.7: Tatrāññatarassa samaṇassa vā brāhmaṇassa vā evaṁ hoti:
root_text.an10.23:6.1: Pāpikā, bhikkhave, icchā neva kāyena pahātabbā no vācāya, paññāya disvā pahātabbā.
root_text.an10.23:6.3: Idha, bhikkhave, ekacco assaddho samāno ‘saddhoti maṁ jāneyyun’ti icchati;
root_text.an10.23:6.13: Ime vuccanti, bhikkhave, dhammā neva kāyena pahātabbā no vācāya, paññāya disvā pahātabbā.
root_text.an10.23:7.1: Tañce, bhikkhave, bhikkhuṁ lobho abhibhuyya iriyati,
root_text.an10.23:7.12: ‘nāyamāyasmā tathā pajānāti yathā pajānato lobho na hoti, tathāhimaṁ āyasmantaṁ lobho abhibhuyya iriyati;
root_text.an10.23:7.13: nāyamāyasmā tathā pajānāti yathā pajānato doso na hoti …
root_text.an10.23:7.21: pāpikā icchā na hoti, tathāhimaṁ āyasmantaṁ pāpikā icchā abhibhuyya iriyatī’ti.
root_text.an10.23:8.1: Tañce, bhikkhave, bhikkhuṁ lobho nābhibhuyya iriyati,
root_text.an10.23:8.12: ‘tathā ayamāyasmā pajānāti yathā pajānato lobho na hoti, tathāhimaṁ āyasmantaṁ lobho nābhibhuyya iriyati;
root_text.an10.23:8.13: tathā ayamāyasmā pajānāti yathā pajānato doso na hoti …
root_text.an10.23:8.21: pāpikā icchā na hoti, tathāhimaṁ āyasmantaṁ pāpikā icchā nābhibhuyya iriyatī’”ti.

translation_text.an10.23:1.1: “Mendicants, there are things that should be given up by the body, not by speech.
translation_text.an10.23:1.2: There are things that should be given up by speech, not by the body.
translation_text.an10.23:1.3: There are things that should be given up neither by the body, nor by speech, but by seeing again and again with wisdom.
translation_text.an10.23:2.1: And what are the things that should be given up by the body, not by speech?
translation_text.an10.23:2.2: It’s when a mendicant has committed a certain unskillful offense by way of body.
translation_text.an10.23:2.3: After examination, sensible spiritual companions say this to them:
translation_text.an10.23:2.4: ‘Venerable, you’ve committed a certain unskillful offense by way of body.
translation_text.an10.23:2.5: Please give up that bad bodily conduct and develop good bodily conduct.’
translation_text.an10.23:2.6: When spoken to by their sensible spiritual companions they give up that bad bodily conduct and develop good bodily conduct.
translation_text.an10.23:2.7: These are the things that should be given up by the body, not by speech.
translation_text.an10.23:3.1: And what are the things that should be given up by speech, not by the body?
translation_text.an10.23:3.2: It’s when a mendicant has committed a certain unskillful offense by way of speech.
translation_text.an10.23:3.3: After examination, sensible spiritual companions say this to them:
translation_text.an10.23:3.4: ‘Venerable, you’ve committed a certain unskillful offense by way of speech.
translation_text.an10.23:3.5: Please give up that bad verbal conduct and develop good verbal conduct.’
translation_text.an10.23:3.6: When spoken to by their sensible spiritual companions they give up that bad verbal conduct and develop good verbal conduct.
translation_text.an10.23:3.7: These are the things that should be given up by speech, not by the body.
translation_text.an10.23:4.1: And what are the things that should be given up neither by the body, nor by speech, but by seeing again and again with wisdom?
translation_text.an10.23:4.9: and stinginess are things that should be given up neither by the body, nor by speech, but by seeing again and again with wisdom.
translation_text.an10.23:5.1: Nasty jealousy should be given up neither by the body, nor by speech, but by seeing again and again with wisdom.
translation_text.an10.23:5.3: It’s when a householder or their child is prospering in money, grain, silver, or gold.
translation_text.an10.23:5.4: And a bondservant or dependent thinks:
translation_text.an10.23:5.5: ‘Oh, may that householder or their child not prosper in money, grain, silver, or gold!’
translation_text.an10.23:5.6: Or an ascetic or brahmin receives robes, almsfood, lodgings, and medicines and supplies for the sick.
translation_text.an10.23:5.7: And some other ascetic or brahmin thinks:
translation_text.an10.23:5.8: ‘Oh, may that venerable not receive robes, almsfood, lodgings, and medicines and supplies for the sick.’
translation_text.an10.23:6.1: Corrupt wishes should be given up neither by the body, nor by speech, but by seeing again and again with wisdom.
translation_text.an10.23:6.3: It’s when a faithless person wishes to be known as faithful.
translation_text.an10.23:6.4: An unethical person wishes to be known as ethical.
translation_text.an10.23:6.5: An unlearned person wishes to be known as learned.
translation_text.an10.23:6.6: A lover of company wishes to be known as secluded.
translation_text.an10.23:6.7: A lazy person wishes to be known as energetic.
translation_text.an10.23:6.8: An unmindful person wishes to be known as mindful.
translation_text.an10.23:6.9: A person without immersion wishes to be known as having immersion.
translation_text.an10.23:6.10: A witless person wishes to be known as wise.
translation_text.an10.23:6.11: Someone who has not ended the defilements wishes to be known as having ended the defilements.
translation_text.an10.23:6.13: Corrupt wishes should be given up neither by the body, nor by speech, but by seeing again and again with wisdom.
translation_text.an10.23:7.1: Suppose that greed masters that mendicant and keeps going.
translation_text.an10.23:7.10: or corrupt wishes master them and keep going.
translation_text.an10.23:7.12: ‘This venerable does not have the understanding that would eliminate greed, so greed masters them and keeps going.
translation_text.an10.23:7.13: They don’t have the understanding that would eliminate hate …
translation_text.an10.23:7.21: or corrupt wishes, so corrupt wishes master them and keep going.’
translation_text.an10.23:8.1: Suppose that greed does not master that mendicant and keep going.
translation_text.an10.23:8.10: or corrupt wishes don’t master that mendicant and keep going.
translation_text.an10.23:8.12: ‘This venerable has the understanding that eliminates greed, so greed doesn’t master them and keep going.
translation_text.an10.23:8.13: They have the understanding that eliminates hate …
translation_text.an10.23:8.21: and corrupt wishes, so corrupt wishes don’t master them and keep going.’”

variant_text.an10.23:2.2: āpanno hoti kiñci desaṁ → kañcideva desaṁ (bj, sya-all, pts1ed)
